docs.summary: Default page and container background.
docs.design: First step in the surface elevation scale. Anchors the layering system — raised surfaces sit above this token, not below.
docs.usage: Use as the background for the primary content area, cards, modals, and any surface that sits at the base elevation. Do not use for text or interactive elements.

semantic.consumerNotes: Foundational surface token. Part of the elevation scale. All raised surfaces sit above this.

docs.design: First step in the surface elevation scale. Anchors the layering system — raised surfaces sit above this token, not below.
docs.summary: Default page and container background.
docs.usage: Use as the background for the primary content area, cards, modals, and any surface that sits at the base elevation. Do not use for text or interactive elements.

semantic.usage: Use as the background for the primary content area, cards, modals, and any surface that sits at the base elevation level.

governance.usage: Use as the background for the primary content area, cards, modals, and any surface that sits at the base elevation level.
governance.consumerNotes: Foundational surface token. Part of the elevation scale. All raised surfaces sit above this.

docs.summary: Elevated surface background, one step above the base layer.
docs.design: Second step in the surface elevation scale. Creates visual separation from surface-base through a subtle warm-grey tint.
docs.usage: Use for cards, panels, and drawers that sit above the base page surface. Do not use for the root page background or inline content areas.

semantic.consumerNotes: New in Cedar v3. Currently under review; expect minor adjustments through Q2 2026.

docs.design: Second step in the surface elevation scale. Creates visual separation from surface-base through a subtle warm-grey tint.
docs.summary: Elevated surface background, one step above the base layer.
docs.usage: Use for cards, panels, and drawers that sit above the base page surface. Do not use for the root page background or inline content areas.

semantic.usage: Use for surfaces that appear elevated above the page background. Typical use: cards, panels, popovers within a primary surface.

governance.usage: Use for surfaces that appear elevated above the page background. Typical use: cards, panels, popovers within a primary surface.
governance.consumerNotes: New in Cedar v3. Currently under review; expect minor adjustments through Q2 2026.

docs.summary: Primary text color for body content and headings.
docs.design: Anchors the text scale at maximum legibility. Darkest step in the warm-grey text ramp.
docs.usage: Use for all default body copy, headings, and labels. Do not use for decorative text, disabled states, or secondary information — use text-subtle for those.

docs.design: Anchors the text scale at maximum legibility. Darkest step in the warm-grey text ramp.
docs.summary: Primary text color for body content and headings.
docs.usage: Use for all default body copy, headings, and labels. Do not use for decorative text, disabled states, or secondary information — use text-subtle for those.

docs.summary: Secondary text color for supporting and supplementary content.
docs.design: Mid-point in the text scale. Reduced contrast signals secondary importance while remaining accessible.
docs.usage: Use for captions, helper text, metadata, and secondary labels. Do not use for primary body copy or anything requiring full legibility — use text-base for those.

docs.design: Mid-point in the text scale. Reduced contrast signals secondary importance while remaining accessible.
docs.summary: Secondary text color for supporting and supplementary content.
docs.usage: Use for captions, helper text, metadata, and secondary labels. Do not use for primary body copy or anything requiring full legibility — use text-base for those.

docs.summary: Text color for interactive links and inline navigation.
docs.design: Draws from the blue brand scale to signal interactivity. Paired with text-link-hover to form the link interaction ramp.
docs.usage: Use for hyperlinks, inline anchors, and any text that navigates or triggers an action. Do not use for non-interactive text — use text-base or text-subtle instead.

docs.design: Draws from the blue brand scale to signal interactivity. Paired with text-link-hover to form the link interaction ramp.
docs.summary: Text color for interactive links and inline navigation.
docs.usage: Use for hyperlinks, inline anchors, and any text that navigates or triggers an action. Do not use for non-interactive text — use text-base or text-subtle instead.

docs.summary: Text color for links in their hovered state.
docs.design: Lighter step in the blue brand scale, creating a visible but controlled shift from text-link on hover.
docs.usage: Use only as the hover state for text-link. Do not use for default link text or any non-interactive element.

docs.design: Lighter step in the blue brand scale, creating a visible but controlled shift from text-link on hover.
docs.summary: Text color for links in their hovered state.
docs.usage: Use only as the hover state for text-link. Do not use for default link text or any non-interactive element.

docs.summary: Text color for sale prices and promotional messaging.
docs.design: Draws from the red brand scale. Reserved solely for commerce pricing contexts to preserve its signal strength.
docs.usage: Use exclusively for sale price labels and discount indicators. Do not use for error states, alerts, or general emphasis — those have dedicated tokens.

docs.design: Draws from the red brand scale. Reserved solely for commerce pricing contexts to preserve its signal strength.
docs.summary: Text color for sale prices and promotional messaging.
docs.usage: Use exclusively for sale price labels and discount indicators. Do not use for error states, alerts, or general emphasis — those have dedicated tokens.

docs.summary: Default border color for containers, inputs, and dividers.
docs.design: Mid-light step in the warm-grey scale, providing enough contrast to define structure without competing with content.
docs.usage: Use for input field borders, card outlines, and structural dividers. Do not use for decorative separators or low-emphasis boundaries — use border-subtle for those.

docs.design: Mid-light step in the warm-grey scale, providing enough contrast to define structure without competing with content.
docs.summary: Default border color for containers, inputs, and dividers.
docs.usage: Use for input field borders, card outlines, and structural dividers. Do not use for decorative separators or low-emphasis boundaries — use border-subtle for those.

docs.summary: Low-emphasis border for decorative separators and understated boundaries.
docs.design: Lightest step in the border scale. Provides just enough separation to guide the eye without drawing attention.
docs.usage: Use for dividers between list items, section rules, and subtle layout separators. Do not use where a border needs to define a structure or interactive boundary — use border-base for those.

docs.design: Lightest step in the border scale. Provides just enough separation to guide the eye without drawing attention.
docs.summary: Low-emphasis border for decorative separators and understated boundaries.
docs.usage: Use for dividers between list items, section rules, and subtle layout separators. Do not use where a border needs to define a structure or interactive boundary — use border-base for those.
